source.title: Structural elucidation of SARS-CoV-2 vital proteins: Computational methods reveal potential drug candidates against main protease, Nsp12 polymerase and Nsp13 helicase

initial_request.research_problem: Urgent need for antivirals against SARS-CoV-2 due to COVID-19 pandemic. No licensed drugs or vaccines available.
initial_request.target_object: SARS-CoV-2 main protease (Mpro), Nsp12 polymerase, Nsp13 helicase
initial_request.known_context: Conserved viral proteins are attractive drug targets. Initial lack of crystal structures; homology models built. Later, crystal structure 6LU7 for Mpro became available.
constraints: No licensed antiviral drugs or vaccines available
input_data: Homology models of Mpro, Nsp12, Nsp13
quantifiable_goals: Identify small molecule inhibitors via virtual screening and MD simulations

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No licensed antiviral drugs or vaccines available for COVID-19.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Urgent need for rapid drug discovery methods.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Computational drug discovery can serve as an alternative to high-throughput screening for hit-to-lead optimization.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Adopt computational methods for virtual screening.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Computational methods provide a rapid alternative to tiresome high-throughput screening.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed to build homology models for target proteins.

evidence.quote_or_span: To date, no licensed antiviral drugs or vaccines are available against COVID-19 although several clinical trials are under way to test possible therapies.
During this urgent situation, computational drug discovery methods provide an alternative to tiresome high-throughput screening, particularly in the hit-to-lead-optimization stage.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No crystal structures available for SARS-CoV-2 Mpro, Nsp12, Nsp13.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Homology models can be built using related viral protein templates.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Generate homology models and refine with MD.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Homology models refined by 20 ns MD to remove steric clashes and optimize geometry.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate Mpro model against available crystal structure.

evidence.quote_or_span: The generated homology models were refined using 20 ns molecular dynamics (MD) simulations to remove steric clashes and optimize side-chain geometry.

evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Homology model should closely match crystal structure.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare Mpro model with crystal structure 6LU7.
evolution_trajectory.observation: RMSD <0.5 Å between homology model and 6LU7, indicating high reliability.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Switch to crystal structure for docking.

evidence.quote_or_span: With the availability of the crystal structure of SARS-CoV-2 Mpro (6LU7) complexed with N3 peptide, homology model was also compared and less than 0.5 Å RMSD was found, which indicated the reliability of the generated model with only subtle difference

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Homology model validated, but crystal structure available.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Crystal structure may provide more accurate docking.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Using co-crystallized structure 6LU7 will improve docking accuracy.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use 6LU7 for Mpro docking instead of homology model.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Co-crystallized Mpro structure (6LU7) utilized for docking.

evidence.quote_or_span: Later for molecular docking studies, a recently reported co-crystallized Mpro structure was utilized (PDB ID: 6LU7).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Structures ready for Mpro, Nsp12, Nsp13.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to identify potential inhibitors from large compound library.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Stepwise virtual screening with ADMET filtering and two-stage docking will yield hits.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Apply virtual screening pipeline: ADMET, AutoDock Vina, Glide XP, MD, MM-GBSA.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Pipeline resulted in 5, 4, and 3 hits for Mpro, Nsp12, and Nsp13 respectively.

evidence.quote_or_span: A stepwise structure-based virtual screening pipeline was adopted considering the putative binding sites of SARS-CoV-2 Mpro, Nsp12 polymerase and Nsp13 helicase to identity potential hits
the subsequent screening resulted in 5, 4 and 3 hits for SARS-CoV-2 Mpro, Nsp12 and Nsp13, respectively

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Many hits may have poor drug-likeness or toxicity.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: ADMET filtering will remove unsuitable compounds.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Substantial hits removed due to renal transporter inhibition, CYP450 effects, and high-risk groups like epoxides/quinones.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed with remaining hits for detailed analysis.

evidence.quote_or_span: This filtering removed substantial hits that exhibited poor ADMET due to inhibitory effects on the renal organic cation transporter and CYP450 isozymes.
a large subset of hits was found to contain high-risk chemical groups like epoxides and quinones

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to select best Mpro inhibitors based on binding and stability.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Hits interacting with catalytic dyad and stable in MD will be promising.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Filter by catalytic dyad interaction, run 20 ns MD, calculate MM-GBSA.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 13 compounds analyzed; 5 selected based on stability and favorable MM-GBSA binding energies.

evidence.quote_or_span: Top-ranked SARS-CoV-2 Mpro hits were filtered based on the interaction with the catalytic dyad (at least one H-bond with either His41 or Cys145 or strong vdW interactions).
A total of 13 compounds were analysed after 20 ns MD simulation based on the stability for at least last 5 ns.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to select best Nsp12 inhibitors.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Hits forming H-bonds with conserved motifs, especially motif C, will be effective.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Select by H-bond with motif C, run 20 ns MD, calculate MM-GBSA.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 17 hits analyzed; 3 selected with stable RMSD and favorable MM-GBSA binding energies.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate Nsp12 model against crystal structure.

evidence.quote_or_span: hits were selected based on establishing H-bond with conserved motifs, especially with signature motif C (SDD)
A total of 17 hits were analysed through 20ns MD simulations, and complexes with stable RMSD were further processed through MMGBSA binding free energy calculations.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Nsp12 hits identified using homology model.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm binding modes with crystal structure.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Docking into crystal structure 6M71 will show identical binding modes.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Identical binding modes observed for cmp2, cmp17a, and cmp21 when docked into the polymerase active site of the crystal structure.

evidence.quote_or_span: the conformations of cmp2, cmp17a and cmp21 showed identical binding modes when docked inside the polymerase active site of crystal structure

Individual compounds of the docking library together with Mcule database were docked iteratively into respective binding pockets of the representative SARS-CoV-2 Mpro, Nsp12 polymerase, and Nsp13 helicase structures.
A stepwise structure-based virtual screening pipeline was adopted considering the putative binding sites of SARS-CoV-2 Mpro, Nsp12 polymerase and Nsp13 helicase to identity potential hits
the subsequent screening resulted in 5, 4 and 3 hits for SARS-CoV-2 Mpro, Nsp12 and Nsp13, respectively

For SARS-CoV-2 Nsp13 helicase, top hits were selected based on significant interactions with six key residues (Lys288, Ser289, Asp374, Glu375, Gln404 and Arg567) including neighbouring residues involved in NTP hydrolysis $[42]$ .

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to select best Nsp13 inhibitors.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Hits interacting with six key NTPase residues will inhibit helicase.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Select by interactions with key residues, run 20 ns MD, calculate MM-GBSA.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 4 compounds identified after MD with favorable MM-GBSA binding energies.

evidence.quote_or_span: top hits were selected based on significant interactions with six key residues (Lys288, Ser289, Asp374, Glu375, Gln404 and Arg567) including neighbouring residues involved in NTP hydrolysis
A 20 ns MD simulation on top ranked complexes led to the identification of 4 best compounds

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Computational hits identified for all three targets.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Identified compounds are promising but require in vitro testing.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Conclude with recommendation for experimental validation.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Study provides structural insights and candidate inhibitors, but experimental validation is needed.

evidence.quote_or_span: Although the current study lacks experimental validation, the structural information obtained from this computational study has paved way for the design of targeted inhibitors to combat COVID-19 outbreak.

this comprehensive study presents an integrated computational approach to the identification of novel inhibitors into the area of anti-nCoV treatment as a starting point, which warrants in vitro testing to evaluate compound efficacy.

validation_methods: Mpro homology model validated against crystal structure 6LU7 (RMSD <0.5 Å)
Nsp12 homology model validated against crystal structure 6M71 (identical binding modes)
Docking protocol validated by redocking N3 peptide into Mpro
success_verification.final_conclusion: Identified 5, 4, and 3 hits for Mpro, Nsp12, and Nsp13 respectively, with favorable binding energies and drug-like properties. Compounds warrant in vitro testing.
limitations: No experimental validation of identified hits
Homology models used for Nsp12 and Nsp13 (though validated later)
ADMET filtering removed many hits due to toxicity risks

risk_notes: All evidence IDs and quotes copied exactly from evidence bank.
